# Generated by ollama (starcoder2)
# Prompt: Create an Istio mesh configuration (meshConfig) that allows outbound traffic to any external service. Set outboundTrafficPolicy mode to ALLOW_ANY so services can call external APIs without ServiceEntry definitions. This provides maximum flexibility for microservices that integrate with various external APIs.
# Category: service_mesh_security

apiVersion: install.istio.io/v1alpha2
kind: IstioOperator
metadata:
  name: istio-operator
spec:
  profile: customizable # Default is default. Choose a mesh profile from the available options in https://istio.io/docs/setup/additional-setup/config-profiles/#mesh-profiles .
  values:
    global:
      useMCP: "false"
      proxyMetadata:
        includeIPRanges: "0.0.0.0/0" # If a pod is running in a virtualized environment, for example on a cloud provider virtual machine and a container runtime using Kata Containers or similar technology (where the root namespace of each container is different), then set this to the value obtained by running the command `ip -br | awk '/eth0/{printf $3}'` on that virtual machine.
      network: istio
      tracing:
        customZipkin: {}
    meshConfig:
      accessLogService: disabled # Disable Zipkin as an Istio-supported observability solution (for simplicity and avoid setting up a cloud provider-specific observability integration). Set to a valid Zipkin host for production scenarios.
      enableTracing: true # Enables trace sampling, the ability to instrument requests and extract distributed tracing context via HTTP headers such as B3 or W3C TraceContext, and configures Istio to export traces in text format. For best results, this requires an existing collector (see https://istio.io/latest/docs/ops/integrations/zipkin/#setting-up-the-collector).
      enablePrometheus: true # Enables Prometheus as an Istio-supported metrics source. Metrics are collected by default for the service mesh, virtual services and destination rules that are in the control plane configuration. A Collector service must be available to serve the metrics. For simplicity this requires an existing collector (see https://istio.io/latest/docs/ops/integrations/prometheus/#setting-up-the-collector).
      enablePolicyChecks: true # Enforces policies specified by Virtual Service and Destination Rule resources in the service mesh configuration. Set to false if these policies are managed separately outside of Istio. If set to false, you must ensure that your rules are compliant with Istio's support for [Open Policy Agent](https://github.com/open-policy-agent/opa), [Crossplane](https://crossplane.io) and other service management capabilities, such as [Sevenguard](https://www.sevenng.eu/) and [Tekton Pipelines](https://tekton.dev/).
    k8sIngress:
      enabled: true # Install Istio Ingress to configure ingress on the cluster. Set to false if external load balancers are used, or internal load balancers are deployed elsewhere in the cluster (for example behind a firewall). If ingress is disabled, you can manually create resources such as Gateway and VirtualService resources to control traffic using Istio.
      ingressClass: istio-ingress # IngressClass specifies which Ingress controller to use for external access. For cloud providers this must be set.
      ingressGateway:
        loadBalancerIP: "172.30.49.65"
        serviceAnnotations:
          service.beta.kubernetes.io/aws-load-balancer-type:nlb # AWS specific configuration (see https://docs.aws.amazon.com/eks/latest/userguide/create-cluster-fargate.html)
    citadel:
      enabled: false # Enables use of Citadel as an alternative to Istio Pilot for identity management and key generation.
    citadel_service_cert_validating_webhook:
      enabled: true # Allows the Citadel service certificate to be validated by a validating webhook. This prevents malicious entities from generating certificates with the identity of the Citadel service.
    egressGateway:
      enabled: false # Enable a gateway for external requests. This is disabled by default if any gateway is enabled in meshConfig and k8sIngress.
    gateways: {} # A map specifying one or more Gateways to be created. You can disable automatic creation of a Gateway resource with `global.gateway.enabled = "false"`.
    sidecarInjectorWebhook:
      enabled: false # Inject sidecars for all workloads in the mesh regardless of workload type (Deployment, StatefulSet and DaemonSet). This feature does not inject Sidecar containers into CronJob and Job resources by default as those use a different mechanism to control execution. You must disable this feature if using `jobs.automountServiceAccountToken` for Jobs or Pods with service accounts in non-default namespaces.
    telemetry:
      enabled: false # Enable configuration of Prometheus, Jaeger and Zipkin as an Istio-supported observability solution (for simplicity and avoid setting up a cloud provider-specific observability integration). Set to false if these tools are managed separately outside of Istio.
      prometheus:
        scrapeInterval: 30s # Defaults to 1m if not specified in custom profiles, otherwise uses the mesh config from global or istio-controlplane values.
        enableConfigAgent: true # The configuration agent will push metrics for all services to Prometheus. It is enabled by default.
        externalUrl: prometheus-metrics.default.svc.cluster.local # If this is not empty, all metrics will be served under this domain. Use the format of http://<domain>:<port>/. Defaults to an internal service that requires an ingress gateway in a mesh. This configuration option is only supported by `pilot_controlplane` profile or custom profiles configured with `values.global.proxy.envoyStatsd.enabled = false`.
      tracer:
        enabled: true # Enables trace sampling, the ability to instrument requests and extract distributed tracing context via HTTP headers such as B3 or W3C TraceContext, and configures Istio to export traces in text format. For best results, this requires an existing collector (see https://istio.io/latest/docs/ops/integrations/zipkin/#setting-up-the-collector).
        provider: zipkin # Options include: Jaeger, OpenTelemetry, Prometheus, Honeycomb and Zipkin
        sampling:
          initial_sampling_percentage: 100 # Set initial sample percentage to 100%. Must be > 0. This configuration option is only supported by `pilot_controlplane` profile or custom profiles configured with `values.global.proxy.envoyStatsd.enabled = false`.
        zipkin:
          address: istio-tracing-istio-zipkin.istio-system.svc.cluster.local:9411 # Address of the tracing service that runs Zipkin. This configuration option is only supported by `pilot_controlplane` profile or custom profiles configured with `values.global.proxy.envoyStatsd.enabled = false`.
    mtls:
      enabled: true # Configures mutual TLS (mTLS) for the service mesh. Mutual TLS is disabled by default to preserve backward compatibility. The ServiceEntries below must enable mTLS for it to work and are not automatically created when Istio is installed using Helm. You should create these ServiceEntry resources outside of the control plane.
    citadel_service_cert_webhook:
      enabled: true # Allows a mutually signed certificate to be used as a trusted CA (CA) and validated by the Citadel service. This prevents malicious entities from generating certificates with the identity of the Citadel service.
- ServiceEntry for Istiod
  - name: istio-ca
  - create: false # Set to true if you do not have `mtls` enabled in Istio. If so, you should generate a CA and populate these fields appropriately (see below) rather than using the default values set here.
  - addresses:
    - 172.30.49.65 # Your cluster's load balancer IP address, obtained by running `kubectl get ingressgateway -n istio-system` after installing Istio. If you are behind a firewall that only allows access from a particular IP range then update this value to match your external IP address or block access altogether using `use_ip_ranges`. If you do not have an ingress controller (for example, if all of your workloads run on Fargate), then you must manually create a VirtualService resource for each workload that requires inbound communication.
    - 172.30.49.65
    - localhost
- ServiceEntry for Prometheus
  - name: prometheus-ca # Change to prometheus-mtls if `enablePrometheus` is set to false in custom profiles or `values.telemetry.prometheus.enabled = true`. This must match the key for certificate that should be validated by Prometheus.
  - create: false # Set to true only when using a Citadel ServiceCertificate with a CA in place that has a private CA key. You should not use this option if `mtls` is enabled and using the default CAs provided by Istio.
  - addresses:
      - 172.30.49.65 # Your cluster's load balancer IP address, obtained by running `kubectl get ingressgateway -n istio-system` after installing Istio. If you are behind a firewall that only allows access from a particular IP range then update this value to match your external IP address or block access altogether using `use_ip_ranges`. If you do not have an ingress controller (for example, if all of your workloads run on Fargate), then you must manually create a VirtualService resource for each workload that requires inbound communication.
      - 172.30.49.65
    - publicKeys: # Change this to match the key for certificate that should be validated by Prometheus when using mTLS.
        - key:
            name: istio-ca-cert
          path: /etc/certs/prometheus-ca-cert/tls.crt
- ServiceEntry for Zipkin
  - name: zipkin-ca # Change this to zipkin-mtls if `enableTracing` is set to false in custom profiles or `values.telemetry.tracer.enabled = true`. This must match the key for certificate that should be validated by Zipkin.
    - create: false
      - addresses:
        - 172.30.49.65 # Your cluster's load balancer IP address, obtained by running `kubectl get ingressgateway -n istio-system` after installing Istio. If you are behind a firewall that only allows access from a particular IP range then update this value to match your external IP address or block access altogether using `use_ip_ranges`. If you do not have an ingress controller (for example, if all of your workloads run on Fargate), then you must manually create a VirtualService resource for each workload that requires inbound communication.
        - 172.30.49.65
      - publicKeys:
          - key:
              name: istio-ca-cert
            path: /etc/certs/istio-ca-cert/tls.crt
